title: Lost in Translation? Not Today!
story: Every September 30, the world celebrates International Translation Day to honor the amazing people who help us understand each other across languages! Translators work behind the scenes to turn books, movies, websites, and important documents from one language into another. Without them, you might never read your favorite manga or watch that cool foreign movie with subtitles. Today, people share multilingual posts online and thank translators for connecting our world.
activity_question: What do translators help us do?
follow_up_question: What would happen if no one could translate between languages?

title: Sip Into September's Spiced Surprise
story: Hot mulled cider fills kitchens with amazing smells every September 30! People heat up apple cider and add warm spices like cinnamon, cloves, and nutmeg to create this cozy drink. Families gather around steaming mugs, sharing stories while the sweet and spicy flavors warm them up from the inside out. Coffee shops and farmers markets serve special mulled cider recipes, and many people post pictures of their colorful, fragrant creations on social media. It's the perfect way to welcome fall weather!
activity_question: What spices are commonly added to mulled cider?
bonus_fact: Mulled cider was popular in colonial America as a warming winter drink.

title: Love People Day: Spread Kindness Everywhere!
story: September 30 is National Love People Day, when everyone focuses on spreading kindness and showing care for others! People celebrate by doing random acts of kindness like helping neighbors, writing thank-you notes, or simply smiling at strangers. Some families volunteer together at local charities or visit elderly relatives they haven't seen in a while. Social media fills with positive messages and heart emojis as people share stories about the good deeds they've witnessed or done themselves.
activity_question: What do people often fill social media with on National Love People Day?
activity_choices: Pictures of their pets wearing hats
follow_up_question: What random act of kindness would make someone's day better?

title: Getting Down and Dirty for Beauty
story: People slather mud all over their faces on September 30 for National Mud Pack Day! This slimy holiday celebrates the ancient beauty trick of using clay and mud to make skin feel smooth and clean. Spas offer special mud treatments, while families make DIY face masks at home using clay powder mixed with water. Some people even post funny selfies of their muddy faces on social media. The mud is supposed to pull dirt out of your pores and leave your skin feeling fresh. It might look gross, but mud pack fans swear it works better than expensive creams!
activity_question: What do people do with mud on National Mud Pack Day?
activity_choices: Put it on their faces as beauty treatments
Build mud castles in their backyards
Use it to paint pictures on walls
activity_answer: Put it on their faces as beauty treatments
follow_up_question: What other unusual ingredients do you think people use for homemade beauty treatments?

title: Orange You Glad It's Orange Shirt Day?
story: People across Canada wear bright orange shirts on September 30 to honor Indigenous children who attended residential schools. Orange Shirt Day started when people learned about Phyllis Webstad, whose new orange shirt was taken away on her first day at residential school. Today, schools, offices, and communities organize events where everyone wears orange to remember these children and learn about their experiences. It's a day for listening, learning, and showing respect.
activity_question: Why do people wear orange shirts on this day?
activity_choices: To honor Indigenous children who attended residential schools
To celebrate the fall harvest season
To support their favorite sports team
activity_answer: To honor Indigenous children who attended residential schools
follow_up_question: What other ways could communities show respect and learn about Indigenous history?

title: Ask a Stupid Question Day Gets Smart
story: Every September 30, teachers and students celebrate Ask a Stupid Question Day by encouraging everyone to ask ANY question without feeling embarrassed! This fun holiday started because teachers noticed kids were afraid to ask questions they thought might sound dumb. On this day, classrooms buzz with curious questions like "Why don't we fall off the Earth?" and "Can fish get thirsty?" Teachers love it because they know there's no such thing as a truly stupid question - only curious minds wanting to learn. Many schools hold special question sessions where students can ask about anything that puzzles them, from science to history to how things work.
activity_question: Why was Ask a Stupid Question Day created?
activity_choices: Because kids were afraid to ask questions they thought sounded dumb
Because teachers ran out of things to teach
Because students were asking too many smart questions
Because schools needed a new holiday to celebrate
activity_answer: Because kids were afraid to ask questions they thought sounded dumb
follow_up_question: What's a question you've always wanted to ask but felt too shy to say out loud?
